originalTitle: Army commander convicted of Guinea stadium massacre dies in prison
title: Army commander convicted of Guinea stadium massacre dies in prison

originalTitle: Senegal to parade Afcon trophy despite title being stripped
title: Senegal's Big Celebration: Cheering for Our Football Heroes!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Welcome to our kid-friendly news report! I'm so excited to share some news with you from our wonderful continent. Today, we're going to talk about football, which is also called soccer! Do you love watching football? It's a game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's net to score a goal. It's so much fun to play and watch, and it makes many people in Africa very happy!

What is AFCON?

We have a very special football competition in Africa called AFCON. AFCON stands for the Africa Cup of Nations. It's like a big, exciting party where all the best football teams from different African countries come together to play. They all want to win the big trophy and be called the champions of Africa! It's a time for cheering, singing, and showing love for our countries. Did you know? AFCON happens every two years! It's like waiting for a special holiday or a big birthday celebration for football in Africa!

Meet Senegal! Our Amazing Lions!

Today, we're talking about a lovely country in West Africa called Senegal. Their national football team is super special! They are called the "Lions of Teranga." "Teranga" is a very important word in Senegal. It means being kind, welcoming, and hospitable to everyone. So, their team plays with big hearts and welcomes challenges! Did you know? Senegal's flag is green, yellow, and red with a green star in the middle! Green stands for hope, yellow for wealth, and red for sacrifice.

A Special Trophy!

Last year, Senegal played incredibly well in AFCON. They worked super hard, ran fast, kicked strongly, and scored many goals! And guess what? They won the big AFCON trophy! A trophy is a shiny, special cup or award you get when you win a big competition. When Senegal won, everyone in their country, and many people across Africa, cheered very loudly! It was a very proud moment for them. Did you know? Trophies are like big, shiny medals, but usually bigger and you can often put them on a shelf to show off your amazing achievement!

A Bit of a Change

Now, here's where things get a little different. Sometimes, in big sports, grown-ups who make the rules have to make difficult decisions. After Senegal won the trophy, there was a new decision made. The official AFCON title was given to another fantastic African team, Morocco. This made some people in Senegal feel a little sad or confused, because they had worked so hard and celebrated their win. It can be a bit like playing a game with your friends, and then the rules change a little bit after you've already won. But it's important to remember that Senegal's players still played wonderfully and showed amazing skill!

Senegal's Big Heart and Celebration!

Even with this change, the people of Senegal have big hearts. They are so proud of their football team, the Lions of Teranga, for how hard they played and how much effort they put in. They believe their team is still champions in their hearts! So, they decided they still want to celebrate their team's hard work, courage, and amazing spirit. Did you know? Even when you don't win the official prize, trying your best and playing with a good spirit is always a victory in itself!

A Super Happy Parade!

To show their love and pride, Senegal is planning a super happy parade! What is a parade? It's a big, fun march where lots of people gather. There's usually music, dancing, colourful flags, and lots of cheering! The team will ride through the streets, and everyone will wave and clap for them. They will show off the trophy they won to all their loving fans. This parade is a way for the people to say, "Thank you! We love you! You are our heroes!" It shows that even if the official decision changed, the joy of playing, the teamwork, and the love for their country remain strong. It's all about celebrating effort and togetherness. Did you know? Parades are a great way for communities to come together, share happiness, and show how proud they are of something special!

Playing a Friendly Game!

After their big parade, the Lions of Teranga will play a "friendly game." A friendly game is a football match that is just for fun and practice. It's not for a big prize like the AFCON trophy, but it's a chance for the team to play together, try new moves, and just enjoy the beautiful game of football. They will be playing against a team from a country called Peru. Peru is a country very far away, across a big ocean! It's lovely when teams from different parts of the world come together to play. It helps everyone learn more about each other and enjoy football even more. Did you know? Friendly games help teams practice new skills and play together without too much pressure! It's like playing a game just for the joy of it!

Where is This Happening?

This special celebration and friendly game will not be in Senegal. It will be happening in a big, famous city called Paris. Paris is in a country far away called France. Many people from Senegal live in Paris too, so it's a wonderful way for the team to celebrate with all their fans, no matter where they are in the world! Did you know? Paris is famous for a very tall and beautiful tower called the Eiffel Tower! It's one of the most famous buildings in the world!

Why is This Important?

This story from Senegal teaches us something very important. It teaches us that even when things don't go exactly as planned, or when decisions are made that we don't understand, we can still choose to be happy and celebrate. It shows us the power of believing in our heroes and cheering for them with all our hearts. It reminds us that football is about more than just winning a cup; it's about teamwork, effort, passion, and bringing people together. So, let's cheer for Senegal, for their strong spirit, and for all our amazing African teams! Football truly brings us all together, like one big, happy African family!
summary: Senegal's football team, the Lions of Teranga, will celebrate their amazing efforts with a big parade and show off the AFCON trophy they won, even though the official title was later given to Morocco.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that even when things are difficult, it's important to celebrate hard work, teamwork, and the love for your country and sport. It teaches us about sportsmanship and finding joy even when rules change.

originalTitle: Costa Rica to accept 25 deportees per week under Trump deportation effort
title: Costa Rica to accept 25 deportees per week under Trump deportation effort
content: The Central American nation is latest to sign a 'third-country' deportation agreement with the Trump administration.

originalTitle: Trump ‘pausing’ plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days
title: Trump ‘pausing’ plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days
content: Donald Trump says he is “pausing” his plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days.

originalTitle: EU’s top diplomat urges US to pressure Russia to stop aiding Iran
title: Big Countries Talking: A Call for Peace!
content:
Jambo, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friend, Reporter Kito, bringing you super important news from our big, beautiful world! I hope you're all having a fantastic day, full of smiles and sunshine. Today, we're going to talk about something that might sound a little grown-up, but it's really about countries being like big neighbors and trying to get along, just like we do with our friends at school or in our villages. It's all about making sure everyone is safe and happy!

A Special Lady from Europe Speaks Up

Imagine you have a big club, and in this club, there are many, many countries. That's a bit like a group called the 'European Union,' or the EU for short. It's like a big family of countries in Europe that work together. Well, there's a very important lady who helps lead this big club. Her name is Madam Kaja Kallas, and she's like the head prefect of this big European family when it comes to talking with other countries far away. Madam Kaja just spoke up, and she asked a very big country called America to do something important. America is like a giant friend in our world's neighborhood. She asked America to talk to another very big country called Russia. It's like asking your older brother to talk to another friend about something important. She wants America to kindly, but very clearly, ask Russia to stop helping another country called Iran in a way that might be making some people a little worried. It's a bit like when you see your friends playing, and one friend is helping another, but maybe that help isn't making everyone feel safe and happy. The goal is always for everyone to feel good and safe. *Did you know? The European Union is made up of many countries, like France, Germany, and Italy! They share ideas and even have special rules to help them work together, just like our classroom rules help us learn together.*

Countries Are Like Big Families

Think of all the countries in the world as one giant family. Each country has its own house, its own food, its own music, and its own special ways of doing things. But just like in any family, sometimes people need to talk to each other to make sure everyone is getting along and no one is feeling left out or unsafe. Madam Kaja, from the European Union family, wants America and Russia to have a serious chat. She hopes that by America talking to Russia, they can help Iran make choices that keep everyone safe and promote peace. Peace means no fighting, no shouting, and everyone living together with kindness and respect. That's what we all want, right? *Did you know? There are almost 200 countries in our world! Each one is unique and has wonderful things to share, from special dances to delicious foods! *

Who Are These Countries? Let's Learn!

Let's learn a little more about these big 'neighbors' in our world family. It's fun to know about different places!

America (The United States)

America is a very, very big country far away, across a huge ocean! It's also called the United States, or the US for short. Imagine a country so big that it has beaches, giant mountains, and big deserts all in one place! Many different kinds of people live there, speaking different languages and sharing different cultures. America is known for many things, like inventing lots of cool technologies and for its big, busy cities. It's a country with a lot of influence, which means when America talks, many other countries listen. That's why Madam Kaja wants America to talk to Russia. *Did you know? America has a giant statue called the Statue of Liberty, which was a gift from another country, France! It stands for freedom and friendship.*

Russia (A Super Big Neighbor)

Now, let's talk about Russia. Wow, Russia is one of the biggest countries in the world! It's so big that it spans across two continents – Europe and Asia! Imagine a country that takes many, many hours to fly across from one end to the other. It has very cold places with lots of snow and also beautiful forests. Russia has a long history and is known for its amazing stories, beautiful buildings, and even for sending the first person into space! Russia is a very powerful country, and its actions are important to many other countries around the globe. *Did you know? Russia has the world's longest railway line, called the Trans-Siberian Railway! It's so long, you could travel on it for a whole week to get from one side of Russia to the other! *

Iran (A Country with Ancient Stories)

Then there's Iran. Iran is a country in a part of the world called the Middle East. It's a land with a very ancient history, full of amazing old cities, beautiful gardens, and stories that are thousands of years old! Imagine a place where people lived in cities built long, long before your great-grandparents were even born! Iran has rich culture and traditions. Like all countries, Iran wants to be strong and safe. Madam Kaja is asking America to talk to Russia about the kind of help Russia gives to Iran, to make sure it's help that brings peace and safety to everyone in the big world family. *Did you know? Iran is home to one of the world's oldest continuous civilizations! People have been living and building amazing things there for thousands and thousands of years! *

What Does "Pressure" Really Mean?

When we hear the word 'pressure,' sometimes it sounds like someone is being mean or pushing. But in this grown-up world of countries talking, 'pressure' just means asking very seriously and finding ways to make someone understand what's important. It's not about fighting; it's about communicating very clearly. It's like when your parents or teachers ask you to do something important, like finishing your chores or studying for a test. They 'pressure' you to help you learn and grow, not to be unkind. They want you to understand the importance of what you are doing. In the same way, America is asked to 'pressure' Russia to help bring understanding and peace.

Working Together for a Peaceful World

Why is all this talking and discussing so important, you ask? Because everyone, everywhere, wants to live in a peaceful world. We all want to feel safe when we walk to school, when we play with our friends, and when we go to sleep at night. Leaders of countries have a big job to make sure their people are safe and that the world is a happy place for all kids to grow up in. When countries talk to each other, even about difficult things, it's a step towards solving problems without fighting. It's like when you have a disagreement with a friend. Instead of shouting, you talk it out, listen to each other, and find a solution so you can play happily again. That's what these big countries are trying to do – talk and find solutions. Our world is like a giant playground. We all share it. And just like on a playground, we need rules, and we need to be kind and fair to each other. When one country helps another, it's important that this help makes the whole playground a better place, not just one corner of it.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are young, we can help make our world a more peaceful place! How? By being good neighbors ourselves! Be kind to your friends, share your toys, listen when others talk, and always try to solve problems with words, not with anger. Every time you show kindness and understanding, you are helping to make our big world a little bit brighter and a lot more peaceful. Remember, even the biggest problems can start to get better when people decide to talk, listen, and work together. Madam Kaja and leaders around the world are trying to do just that – make our world a safer, happier home for all of us. That's all for now, my wonderful young friends! Keep learning, keep smiling, and keep being the amazing, shining stars you are! Until next time, this is Reporter Kito, signing off from our beautiful Africa!
summary: A leading lady from European countries wants America to strongly ask Russia to stop helping Iran in ways that worry other countries, hoping for peace and safety for everyone.
whyMatters: It matters because when countries talk about their problems, they can find ways to keep everyone safe and stop fighting, helping to make our world a happy home for all of us.

originalTitle: Tonali and Kean end Northern Ireland's World Cup dreams
title: Tonali and Kean end Northern Ireland's World Cup dreams
content: Northern Ireland's hopes of reaching the 2026 World Cup are brought to an end, as they're beaten 2-0 by Italy in their World Cup play-off semi-final in Bergamo, as the home side close in on qualifying for their first World Cup in 12 years.

originalTitle: O'Neill positive about Northern Ireland future despite Italy defeat
title: O'Neill positive about Northern Ireland future despite Italy defeat
content: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill insists the future is bright for his side after they lose their World Cup play-off semi-final against Italy, as they're beaten 2-0 in Bergamo.

originalTitle: Gyokeres Sweden's hero as Kosovo dream of first World Cup
title: Gyokeres Sweden's hero as Kosovo dream of first World Cup
content: Graham Potter's Sweden are one win from qualifying for the World Cup after Viktor Gyokeres scores a hat-trick in their 3-1 play-off semi-final win over Ukraine.
